subject: OceanGate's Titan submersible: how a culture that treated safety as optional dived, warned and warned again, to the bottom of the sea
central_question: Five intelligent people sealed themselves inside an uncertified, experimental submersible that engineers and an entire industry had warned would fail. Why did they believe it would be fine — and what does that belief say about us?
viewer_promise: By the end, the viewer understands the full chain of warnings the Titan ignored, what the U.S. Coast Guard found, and why the disaster was officially judged preventable — a study of how the worship of the rule-breaking visionary can override warning after warning until physics forecloses the choice.
surprise: The four-day search the entire world held its breath for was, investigators concluded, a search for people who had already died on the first day — and the deeper shock is that nearly everyone who mattered had been warned, in writing, for years.
stakes: When does 'move fast and break things' stop being innovation and become the predictable killing of the people who trusted you? The question under every founder who calls safety a waste.
target_audience: English-speaking US general audience interested in disasters, hubris, business, engineering, and how warnings get ignored
demand_evidence: The Titan implosion was one of the most-followed news events of 2023; sustained, high-search, advertiser-safe interest with a clear moral arc.
Recent, self-relevant ('why did smart people believe him?'), strong flagship; the 2025 Coast Guard report ('preventable') gives an authoritative, documentary-grade spine.
competition: Heavily covered as breaking news; few retellings unify the full warning chain (Lochridge + MTS + ignored hull anomalies) with the official 'preventable' finding into one feature-length tragedy centered on belief, not schadenfreude.
differentiation: First PD FEATURE (60 min). Treats a thriller premise as a Greek tragedy: the ending is known, so suspense becomes 'why was it not stopped?' Implicates the audience's own worship of the visionary founder. Strict record-based, dignity-first.
research_feasibility: High: U.S. Coast Guard MBI report (2025), hearing exhibits (incl. the 2018 MTS letter), 2018 court filings (Lochridge), and major-outlet reporting are extensive and authoritative.
visual_feasibility: High via symbolic AI imagery + factory ocean/abyss b-roll + code graphics: a sealed hatch, a tiny sub in the abyss, ink-in-water, a game controller, a Rubik's Cube, sonar screens, the Titanic wreck (symbolic), empty chairs. No real-person likeness (invariant 11).
risk_notes: R2 — recent disaster with named, deceased victims and living relatives. Pre-publish fact re-check REQUIRED (current event; verify USCG report wording, the MTS exhibit URL, and the exact source/wording of the Rush quote).

Dignity is the safeguard: the moral center is Suleman (19). No schadenfreude, no 'they deserved it.' No graphic depiction of the implosion or remains; the implosion was near-instantaneous (no suffering implied).

Living relatives (e.g., Christine Dawood) and the whistleblower (David Lochridge) represented ONLY via documented public statements; no invented dialogue. Attribute the boy's reported anxiety as 'reportedly.'

Attribute CAUSE to the U.S. Coast Guard Board, not as the narrator's independent assertion. Stockton Rush is deceased (not subject to defamation) but is not reduced to a cartoon villain — show the logic of his belief to implicate the audience.
No real-person likeness or deepfake of any of the five, or of Rush (invariant 11). All AI visuals symbolic and disclosed; brand markings (OceanGate/Logitech/Titanic operators) kept non-specific.
FEATURE format (60 min) requires the new 'feature' duration profile in the validator; do NOT silently weaken validation (invariant 15) — add an explicit profile + ADR.
kill_conditions: Cannot sustain strict record-based neutrality and dignity; or the feature-length script cannot keep retention without sensationalizing the deaths; or core facts cannot be re-verified to USCG/court records plus independent reporting at publish time.
